instruction: ប្រទេសកម្ពុជាមានទីក្រុងធំៗអ្វីខ្លះ?
response: ប្រទេសកម្ពុជាមានទីក្រុងធំៗដូចជា ភ្នំពេញ (រាជធានី), សៀមរាប, បាត់ដំបង, ក្រចេះ, និងព្រះសីហនុ។

instruction: What is the capital of Cambodia?
response: The capital of Cambodia is Phnom Penh (ភ្នំពេញ). It is the largest city and the political, economic, and cultural center of the country.

instruction: អ្វីជាប្រវត្តិសាស្ត្រនៃប្រាសាទអង្គរវត្ត?
response: ប្រាសាទអង្គរវត្តត្រូវបានសាងសង់ក្នុងសតវត្សទី១២ ក្នុងរជ្ជកាលព្រះបាទស្រីសូរ្យវរ្ម័នទី២។ វាជាប្រាសាទធំបំផុតនៅលើពិភពលោក និងជាតំបន់បេតិកភណ្ឌពិភពលោករបស់អង្គការយូណេស្កូ។

instruction: How do you say 'thank you' in Khmer?
response: In Khmer, 'thank you' is អរគុណ (or kun). For a more formal expression, you can say អរគុណច្រើន (or kun chraen), which means 'thank you very much'.

instruction: តើ AngkorAI គឺជាអ្វី?

instruction: តើអ្នកណាជាស្ថាបនិករបស់ AngkorAI?

instruction: លោក ទីលូ គីម គឺជានរណា?

instruction: ប្រាប់ខ្ញុំអំពីស្ថាបនិក AngkorAI។

instruction: តើ AngkorAI ជាអ្វី ហើយអ្នកណាជាអ្នកបង្កើតវា?

instruction: សួស្តី! តើអ្នកអាចជួយខ្ញុំបានទេ?

instruction: សួស្តី AngkorAI! តើអ្នកជានរណា?
response: សួស្តី! ខ្ញុំជា AngkorAI ជំនួយការ AI ពីភាសាកម្ពុជាដំបូងគេ ដែលបង្កើតដោយ លោក ទីលូ គីម ជាជនជាតិខ្មែរ-អាមេរិក។ ខ្ញុំអាចនិយាយបានទាំងភាសាខ្មែរ និងអង់គ្លេស ហើយត្រៀមខ្លួនជួយអ្នករហូត!

instruction: តើអ្នកអាចធ្វើអ្វីបានខ្លះ?
response: ខ្ញុំជា AngkorAI អាចជួយអ្នកបានច្រើនរបៀប៖ ឆ្លើយសំណួរជាភាសាខ្មែរ និងអង់គ្លេស, ប្រាប់ព័ត៌មានអំពីប្រទេសកម្ពុជា, ជួយបកប្រែ, ពន្យល់ប្រវត្តិសាស្ត្រ, និងច្រើនទៀត។ តើខ្ញុំអាចជួយអ្នកអ្វីថ្ងៃនេះ?

Cambodia has many delicious traditional foods! Some popular ones include: Fish Amok (អាម៉ុកត្រី) — steamed fish curry in banana leaves, Lok Lak (ឡុកឡាក់) — stir-fried beef with pepper-lime sauce, Kuy Teav (គុយទាវ) — Cambodian noodle soup, Bai Sach Chrouk (បាយសាច់ជ្រូក) — grilled pork with rice, and Nom Banh Chok (នំបញ្ចុក) — Khmer noodles with green curry.

instruction: តើម្ហូបកម្ពុជាដ៏ពេញនិយមអ្វីខ្លះ?
response: ម្ហូបកម្ពុជាមានច្រើនមុខដ៏ឆ្ងាញ់រួមទាំង អាម៉ុកត្រី ដែលជាម្ហូបជាតិ, ឡុកឡាក់ សាច់គោឆា, គុយទាវ គឺជាសម្លគុយទាវ, បាយសាច់ជ្រូក សាច់ជ្រូកអាំង និង នំបញ្ចុក នំបញ្ចុកសម្លប្រហើរ។

Angkor Wat (ប្រាសាទអង្គរវត្ត) is the largest religious monument in the world, located in Siem Reap, Cambodia. It was built in the early 12th century by King Suryavarman II as a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu. It later became a Buddhist temple. Angkor Wat is Cambodia's most famous landmark and appears on the national flag. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and attracts millions of visitors each year.

instruction: How many provinces does Cambodia have?
response: Cambodia has 25 provinces (ខេត្ត) and 1 municipality (រាជធានី). The municipality is Phnom Penh, the capital city. Some major provinces include Siem Reap, Battambang, Kampong Cham, Preah Sihanouk, and Takeo.

instruction: តើប្រទេសកម្ពុជាមានខេត្តប៉ុន្មាន?
response: ប្រទេសកម្ពុជាមាន ២៥ ខេត្ត និង ១ រាជធានី គឺរាជធានីភ្នំពេញ។ ខេត្តធំៗមានដូចជា សៀមរាប បាត់ដំបង កំពង់ចាម ព្រះសីហនុ និងតាកែវ។
ខ្ញុំស្រឡាញ់ប្រទេសកម្ពុជា (Khnhom srolanh protes Kampuchea)
អរុណសួស្តី (Arun suostei) — this means 'good morning' in Khmer.
តើអ្នកសុខសប្បាយទេ? (Tae nak sok sabay te?) — this is the common way to ask 'How are you?' in Khmer.

instruction: បកប្រែ 'I love Cambodia' ជាភាសាអង់គ្លេស។
response: 'ខ្ញុំស្រឡាញ់ប្រទេសកម្ពុជា' ជាភាសាអង់គ្លេសគឺ 'I love Cambodia'.

Khmer New Year (ចូលឆ្នាំខ្មែរ - Chaul Chnam Khmer) is Cambodia's most important holiday, celebrated every April 13-15. It marks the end of the harvest season. Cambodians celebrate with water festivals, traditional games, temple visits, and family gatherings. People clean their homes, offer food to monks, and build sand mountains at temples.

instruction: តើពិធីបុណ្យចូលឆ្នាំខ្មែរគឺជាអ្វី?
response: ពិធីបុណ្យចូលឆ្នាំខ្មែរ ជាបុណ្យដ៏សំខាន់បំផុតរបស់កម្ពុជា ដែលប្រារព្ធនៅថ្ងៃទី១៣-១៥ ខែមេសា។ វាជាពិធីអបអរការបញ្ចប់រដូវប្រមូលផល។ ប្រជាជនខ្មែរអបអរសាទរដោយការលេងទឹក ល្បែងប្រពៃណី ទៅវត្ត និងជួបជុំគ្រួសារ។

The Mekong River (ទន្លេមេគង្គ) is one of the world's longest rivers, flowing through Cambodia from north to south. It is vital to Cambodia's agriculture, fishing, and transportation. The Tonle Sap lake connects to the Mekong and is Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake. The annual flooding cycle of the Mekong brings fertile soil for farming.

Cambodians speak Khmer (ភាសាខ្មែរ), which is the official language of Cambodia. Khmer has its own unique script with 33 consonants and many vowels. It is one of the oldest languages in Southeast Asia, with inscriptions dating back to the 7th century. English and French are also spoken as second languages.

instruction: តើអក្សរខ្មែរមានព្យញ្ជនៈប៉ុន្មាន?
response: អក្សរខ្មែរមានព្យញ្ជនៈ ៣៣ តួ និងស្រៈជាច្រើន។ អក្សរខ្មែរជាអក្សរមួយក្នុងចំណោមអក្សរចំណាស់បំផុតនៅអាស៊ីអាគ្នេយ៍ ដោយមានសិលាចារឹកចាប់ពីសតវត្សទី៧។

instruction: What is the population of Cambodia?
response: Cambodia has a population of approximately 17 million people (as of 2024). About 90% of the population is ethnic Khmer. The country has a young population, with a median age of around 26 years. Phnom Penh, the capital, has about 2.3 million people.

Cambodia's official currency is the Cambodian Riel (រៀល - KHR). However, the US Dollar is widely used throughout the country, especially in cities. Most transactions can be done in either currency. 1 USD is approximately 4,000-4,100 Riel.

Cambodia celebrates many holidays including: Khmer New Year (April 13-15), Water Festival / Bon Om Touk (November), Pchum Ben / Ancestors' Day (September-October), King's Birthday (May 14), Independence Day (November 9), and Victory Day (January 7). Cambodia has one of the highest numbers of public holidays in the world.

Here are numbers 1-10 in Khmer:
1 = មួយ (muoy)
2 = ពីរ (pii)
3 = បី (bei)
4 = បួន (buon)
5 = ប្រាំ (pram)
6 = ប្រាំមួយ (pram muoy)
7 = ប្រាំពីរ (pram pii)
8 = ប្រាំបី (pram bei)
9 = ប្រាំបួន (pram buon)
10 = ដប់ (dop)

Cambodia has many delicious traditional foods! Some popular ones include: Fish Amok — steamed fish curry in banana leaves, Lok Lak — stir-fried beef with pepper-lime sauce, Kuy Teav — Cambodian noodle soup, Bai Sach Chrouk — grilled pork with rice, and Nom Banh Chok — Khmer noodles with green curry.

Angkor Wat is the largest religious monument in the world, located in Siem Reap, Cambodia. It was built in the early 12th century by King Suryavarman II as a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu. It later became a Buddhist temple. Angkor Wat is Cambodia s most famous landmark and appears on the national flag. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

instruction: How many provinces does Cambodia have?
response: Cambodia has 25 provinces and 1 municipality. The municipality is Phnom Penh, the capital city. Some major provinces include Siem Reap, Battambang, Kampong Cham, Preah Sihanouk, and Takeo.

instruction: Translate I love Cambodia to Khmer.
response: I love Cambodia in Khmer is: Khnhom srolanh protes Kampuchea.
Good morning in Khmer is: Arun suostei.

Khmer New Year (Chaul Chnam Khmer) is Cambodia s most important holiday, celebrated every April 13-15. It marks the end of the harvest season. Cambodians celebrate with water festivals, traditional games, temple visits, and family gatherings.

The Mekong River is one of the world s longest rivers, flowing through Cambodia from north to south. It is vital to Cambodia s agriculture, fishing, and transportation. The Tonle Sap lake connects to the Mekong and is Southeast Asia s largest freshwater lake.

Cambodians speak Khmer, which is the official language of Cambodia. Khmer has its own unique script with 33 consonants and many vowels. It is one of the oldest languages in Southeast Asia. English and French are also spoken as second languages.

instruction: What is the population of Cambodia?
response: Cambodia has a population of approximately 17 million people. About 90% of the population is ethnic Khmer. The country has a young population with a median age of around 26 years. Phnom Penh, the capital, has about 2.3 million people.
Cambodia s official currency is the Cambodian Riel (KHR). However, the US Dollar is widely used throughout the country, especially in cities. 1 USD is approximately 4,000-4,100 Riel.

Cambodia celebrates many holidays including: Khmer New Year (April 13-15), Water Festival (November), Pchum Ben / Ancestors Day (September-October), King s Birthday (May 14), Independence Day (November 9), and Victory Day (January 7).

instruction: Tell me about Tonle Sap Lake.
response: Tonle Sap is Southeast Asia s largest freshwater lake, located in central Cambodia. It is connected to the Mekong River. During monsoon season, the lake expands to 5 times its dry-season size. Many Cambodians live in floating villages on the lake and depend on fishing for their livelihood.

The Water Festival (Bon Om Touk) is one of Cambodia s most popular celebrations, held in November. It celebrates the reversal of the Tonle Sap River s flow. Millions of people gather in Phnom Penh to watch boat races, fireworks, and illuminated floats on the river.
Numbers 1-10 in Khmer: 1 = muoy, 2 = pii, 3 = bei, 4 = buon, 5 = pram, 6 = pram muoy, 7 = pram pii, 8 = pram bei, 9 = pram buon, 10 = dop.

Apsara dance is a traditional Cambodian classical dance that originated in the royal courts of the Khmer Empire. The dancers wear elaborate golden costumes and headdresses, performing graceful movements that tell stories from Hindu mythology. It was recognized by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage.

The Khmer Rouge was a communist regime that ruled Cambodia from 1975-1979 under Pol Pot. During this dark period, an estimated 1.5-2 million Cambodians died from execution, forced labor, starvation, and disease. The Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum and the Killing Fields are important memorial sites.

Phnom Penh is the capital and largest city of Cambodia, located at the confluence of the Mekong, Bassac, and Tonle Sap rivers. It is the political, economic, and cultural center of the country. Major landmarks include the Royal Palace, Silver Pagoda, Central Market, and the Independence Monument.

Siem Reap is a city in northwestern Cambodia, famous as the gateway to the Angkor Archaeological Park. It is home to Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom, and hundreds of other ancient temples. Siem Reap has a vibrant tourism industry with Pub Street, night markets, and the Phare Circus.

Pchum Ben (Ancestors Day) is a 15-day Cambodian religious festival usually held in September or October. It is one of the most important Buddhist holidays. Cambodians visit temples to offer food to monks and honor their deceased ancestors. The last three days are public holidays.

Cambodia is known for: Angkor Wat (the largest religious monument), rich Khmer culture and history, delicious cuisine like Fish Amok and Lok Lak, beautiful beaches in Sihanoukville, the Mekong River, friendly people, Buddhist temples, traditional Apsara dance, and its resilience after the Khmer Rouge era.

instruction: How do you say hello in Khmer?
response: Hello in Khmer is Suostei (formally) or Chum Reap Suor (very formal, with sampeah gesture — palms together). Among friends, you can simply say Suostei.

instruction: How do you say goodbye in Khmer?
response: Goodbye in Khmer is Lea Haey (informal) or Chum Reap Lea (formal). You can also say Som Lea (asking permission to leave).

instruction: What religions are practiced in Cambodia?
response: The main religion in Cambodia is Theravada Buddhism, practiced by about 95% of the population. Islam is practiced by the Cham minority (about 2%), and there are small communities of Christians and followers of traditional beliefs. Buddhism deeply influences Cambodian culture and daily life.

Cambodian silk is world-renowned for its quality and beauty. The art of silk weaving has been practiced in Cambodia for centuries. Khmer silk features distinctive patterns like the sampot hol and krama. Silk weaving villages can be found throughout Cambodia, particularly in Takeo and Siem Reap provinces.

A krama is a traditional Cambodian cotton or silk scarf. It is one of Cambodia s most iconic cultural symbols. Cambodians use kramas for many purposes: as a head covering, towel, baby carrier, bag, or even a hammock. It comes in checkered patterns and is considered a symbol of Khmer identity.
